title: AI startups raise funds
dek: AI law and car bidding startups secure funding
meta_description: AI startups secure funding for law and car bidding platforms
body_html:
As the world of artificial intelligence continues to evolve, startups in the space are securing significant funding to bring their innovative ideas to life. One such startup, Norm, has raised $120 million in a Series C round, valuing the company at $1.2 billion. AI law is a growing field, and Norm is at the forefront of this movement.

Norm raises $120M

According to a report by Startups | TechCrunch, Norm has raised a $120 million Series C round led by Khosla Ventures, valuing the startup at $1.2 billion. This significant funding round is a testament to the growing importance of AI law in the startup world. As reported by Startups | TechCrunch, this funding will likely be used to further develop Norm's AI-powered legal tools.

The background of AI law is rooted in the need for more efficient and effective legal processes. Traditional legal systems can be slow and cumbersome, and AI has the potential to revolutionize this field. With the help of machine learning algorithms, legal professionals can analyze vast amounts of data, identify patterns, and make more informed decisions.

As the use of AI in law continues to grow, it is likely that we will see more startups like Norm emerge. The potential applications of AI law are vast, and it will be exciting to see how this field develops in the coming years. According to Startups | TechCrunch, Norm's funding round is a significant milestone in the development of AI law.

In the future, we can expect to see more AI-powered legal tools being developed. These tools will have the potential to transform the legal industry, making it more efficient and effective. As reported by Startups | TechCrunch, Norm is already making significant strides in this area, and their funding round will likely accelerate this process.

Anthropic brings Claude Cowork to mobile and web

Anthropic has launched Claude Cowork on mobile and web, expanding a tool that has quietly become the company's bridge between the developer-centric world of AI coding agents and the far larger market of knowledge workers who never open a terminal. According to VentureBeat, this rollout begins in beta with Max subscribers before expanding to additional plans.

The background of AI coding agents is rooted in the need for more efficient and effective coding processes. Traditional coding methods can be time-consuming and labor-intensive, and AI has the potential to revolutionize this field. With the help of machine learning algorithms, developers can analyze vast amounts of data, identify patterns, and make more informed decisions.

As the use of AI in coding continues to grow, it is likely that we will see more tools like Claude Cowork emerge. The potential applications of AI coding agents are vast, and it will be exciting to see how this field develops in the coming years. According to VentureBeat, Anthropic's launch of Claude Cowork on mobile and web is a significant milestone in the development of AI coding agents.

In the future, we can expect to see more AI-powered coding tools being developed. These tools will have the potential to transform the coding industry, making it more efficient and effective. As reported by VentureBeat, Anthropic is already making significant strides in this area, and their launch of Claude Cowork on mobile and web will likely accelerate this process.

Digital-native startups ditch rigid databases

Digital-native startups are ditching rigid databases for their agentic stacks, according to a report by VentureBeat. The gap between what AI models and agents can produce and what legacy infrastructure can reliably support is known as architectural drag, and it is the defining bottleneck of the agentic era.

The background of agentic stacks is rooted in the need for more flexible and scalable infrastructure. Traditional databases can be inflexible and unable to handle the demands of AI models and agents. With the help of cloud-based databases, startups can analyze vast amounts of data, identify patterns, and make more informed decisions.

As the use of AI in startups continues to grow, it is likely that we will see more companies ditching rigid databases for their agentic stacks. The potential applications of agentic stacks are vast, and it will be exciting to see how this field develops in the coming years. According to VentureBeat, digital-native startups are already making significant strides in this area.

In the future, we can expect to see more cloud-based databases being developed. These databases will have the potential to transform the startup industry, making it more efficient and effective. As reported by VentureBeat, digital-native startups are already benefiting from the use of agentic stacks, and this trend is likely to continue.

Savi’s app aims to protect consumers from AI scams

Savi’s app aims to protect consumers from realistic AI scams like kidnappers demanding ransom. According to Startups | TechCrunch, the company has raised $7 million in seed funding and is launching its app for iPhone and Android.

The background of AI scams is rooted in the growing use of AI in everyday life. As AI becomes more prevalent, the potential for scams and fraud increases. With the help of AI-powered tools, consumers can be protected from these scams and stay safe online.

As the use of AI in everyday life continues to grow, it is likely that we will see more companies like Savi emerging. The potential applications of AI-powered tools are vast, and it will be exciting to see how this field develops in the coming years. According to Startups | TechCrunch, Savi’s app is a significant milestone in the development of AI-powered tools for consumer protection.

In the future, we can expect to see more AI-powered tools being developed to protect consumers from scams. These tools will have the potential to transform the way we stay safe online, making it more efficient and effective. As reported by Startups | TechCrunch, Savi is already making significant strides in this area, and their app launch will likely accelerate this process.

Bidbus pits dealerships against each other

Bidbus, which lets dealerships bid on used cars, has raised $15 million in a Series A round that was led by early-stage mobility fund Ibex Investors. According to Startups | TechCrunch, this funding round will be used to further develop Bidbus’s platform.

The background of used car sales is rooted in the need for more efficient and effective sales processes. Traditional used car sales can be time-consuming and labor-intensive, and AI has the potential to revolutionize this field. With the help of machine learning algorithms, dealerships can analyze vast amounts of data, identify patterns, and make more informed decisions.

As the use of AI in used car sales continues to grow, it is likely that we will see more companies like Bidbus emerge. The potential applications of AI-powered sales tools are vast, and it will be exciting to see how this field develops in the coming years. According to Startups | TechCrunch, Bidbus’s funding round is a significant milestone in the development of AI-powered sales tools.

In the future, we can expect to see more AI-powered sales tools being developed. These tools will have the potential to transform the used car sales industry, making it more efficient and effective. As reported by Startups | TechCrunch, Bidbus is already making significant strides in this area, and their funding round will likely accelerate this process.

The bottom line

In conclusion, the world of AI startups is rapidly evolving, with companies like Norm, Anthropic, and Bidbus securing significant funding to bring their innovative ideas to life. As the use of AI continues to grow, it is likely that we will see more companies emerging in this space.

  • AI law is a growing field, with significant potential for innovation and disruption.
  • AI coding agents are transforming the coding industry, making it more efficient and effective.
  • Agentic stacks are becoming increasingly important for digital-native startups, providing a flexible and scalable infrastructure for AI models and agents.
  • AI-powered tools are being developed to protect consumers from scams and stay safe online.
  • AI-powered sales tools are transforming the used car sales industry, making it more efficient and effective.
